notes: Shot brief captured from the portfolio asset planning conversation. These are the target visual directions for categories 4 through 8.

shots.title: p95 latency graph
shots.notes: Use strong measured numbers and show them prominently.

shots.title: Load test results
shots.notes: Prefer real benchmark tooling such as k6, wrk, or ghz.

shots.title: Grafana dashboard
shots.notes: Use real panel titles and metrics where possible.

shots.title: Terminal benchmark output
shots.notes: Show real commands and results if available.

shots.title: Real-time delay dashboard
shots.notes: Use live dashboard or streaming update visuals.

shots.title: Failover / redundancy diagram
shots.notes: Best fit: distributed rate limiter replicas behind Envoy.

shots.title: Retry / backoff logic flow
shots.notes: Highlight graceful retries instead of brittle failure.

shots.title: Chaos testing screenshot
shots.notes: Best fit: chaos controls in the rate limiter debug dashboard.

shots.title: Error handling logs
shots.notes: Prefer structured logs with fallback / retry context.

shots.title: Queue buffering
shots.notes: Use Redis Streams or SQS where possible.

shots.title: Amazon CloudWatch dashboard
shots.notes: Very strong fit for AWS / logger work.

shots.title: Structured logs
shots.notes: Prefer Lambda or backend logger output.

shots.title: Metrics dashboard
shots.notes: Can use Prometheus or Grafana if CloudWatch is not the only source.

shots.title: Tracing
shots.notes: Use Tempo or trace-like flow if available.

shots.title: Custom metrics
shots.notes: Use explicit metric names rather than generic labels.

shots.title: Throughput graph
shots.notes: Best fit: the 12K rps rate limiter.

shots.title: DB query optimization
shots.notes: Prefer real SQL or PostgreSQL optimization output.

shots.title: Redis hit rate / cache usage
shots.notes: Use Redis stats if available.

shots.title: CPU / memory improvements
shots.notes: Use runtime or profiling output where possible.

shots.title: Streaming performance metrics
shots.notes: Visualize sustained throughput clearly.

categories.title: Debugging
categories.focus: how you find and fix problems

shots.title: Debug logs showing issue
shots.notes: Show the failure signal clearly.

shots.title: Wireshark / packet inspection
shots.notes: Very strong fit if real captures exist.

shots.title: Step-by-step trace
shots.notes: Use service trace or explicit sequence path.

shots.title: Console debugging session
shots.notes: Prefer real terminal context over generic code snippets.

shots.title: Flame graph / profiler
shots.notes: Huge bonus if a real profiler artifact exists.
